task:
Open /home/user/Documents/regional_sales.xlsx in LibreOffice Calc. The 'Sales' sheet has bold headers in row 1 (A='Region', B='Q1', C='Q2', D='Q3', E='Q4', F='Total') and 12 regions in rows 2-13. F2:F13 already contain `=SUM(B2:E2)`-style formulas.

Apply two conditional-formatting rules:
- On range B2:E13: a Color Scale (2- or 3-color, red-to-green) so low values shade red and high values shade green.
- On range F2:F13: a 'Cell value is > 100000' rule that applies a cell style with RED font color and BOLD weight (you may create a new style called 'HighTotal' or reuse the built-in 'Bad' style).

Save the file in xlsx format back to /home/user/Documents/regional_sales.xlsx.
